title: Leucanthemum bauhini
intro: Leucanthemum bauhinii, commonly known as the Oxford daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum bauhinii include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Leucanthemum bauhinii is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.

uses: Leucanthemum bauhini is a lesser-known species of the daisy family, and its medicinal uses have not been extensively documented. properties: Leucanthemum bauhini, also known as the Pyrenean oxeye daisy, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties due to its composition of bioactive compounds.
